betterquesting:10.desc:8:
One generator per machine gets pretty annoying, and it can be hard to generate your first MV amp when you're working with LV components and infrastructure. Luckily, Greg has a solution to this – transformers. An MV transformer (crafted from LV parts) will take 4 amps of LV and output 1 amp of MV, or intake 1 amp of MV and output 4 amps of LV. Transformers can be used in chains to power 16 machines with one generator. 

In addition, energynet has some… broken mechanics. Energy limits are calculated based on input, so you can have 50 generators on a 1amp line, and 50 machines drawing one amp each. As long as you never input more than the amperage of the cables from 1 individual source, you can abuse this mechanic to make lines of machines that share their energy sources, vastly reducing the total amount of generators you need.
